item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Go for it on both counts, so long the word count is not changed.

i.e. In-line links will do.

As for Saturn Deer, perhaps he can fit in the following bolded portion:

Got the short end of the family inheritance? Want to invest in lands beyond the third dimension? Greener pastures are infinite! We at Heavenly Antlers Properties provide a wide range of services to guarantee trans-dimensional acquisition and ownership of land.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Okay, so I generally try to stay hands of with SC2015 articles due to length sensitivity, but there is one place we can do a simple in-line link. Turn the mention of Extranormal Events into a link to the Log of Extranormal Events. I would argue that since Heavenly Antlers Properties is designed to be a reference to Saturn Deer a link could be made there as well, but that might also spoil the Easter egg too much. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Occurring since ██/██/1992, 2312-Eris Events were initially classified as individual Extranormal Events. They were classified under SCP-2312 on ██/██/1998, upon discovering a causality between SCP-2312 manifestations and 2312-Eris Events.

To this:

Occurring since ██/██/1992, 2312-Eris Events were initially classified as individual Extranormal Events. They were classified under SCP-2312 on ██/██/1998, upon discovering a causality between SCP-2312 manifestations and 2312-Eris Events.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: Re: Word Count Note, Thanks, Attribution and Other Matters
item.content:encoded: I appreciate explanations on SCPs like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, it was a Turkish island right from the start. But yeah, it does factor in with the years it took to get the experiment underway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the record, I added Greece to the mix upon a reviewer's advice since the act might have repercussions (said reviewer highlighted "act of aggression against Greece").

I think that might have been me. My recollection was that the draft in question had sovereignty over a Greek island being given to TNRC, but I may have misread it. In any case, I suspect Greece would be concerned about any action to increase the size of the TNRC, even if it's at Turkey's expense - I certainly prefer it being a Turkish island originally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Greece was consulted, but probably out of courtesy, since it wasn't any of their business.

For the record, I added Greece to the mix upon a reviewer's advice since the act might have repercussions (said reviewer highlighted "act of aggression against Greece"). I added Cyprus itself since it falls along that line of reasoning.

But yeah, it's largely out of courtesy and a sense of "there's no other meaning behind us (the Foundation) asking Turkey give up one of its islands for an unrecognised state. It's only because of this weird thing in the mountains that we must figure out".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

Even though I doubt very much that anyone would be able to get either Greece or Turkey at the moment to willingly give up sovereignty of anything for any period for any reason on Cyprus short of armed force

Mmh. My understanding is that the Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus took over an uninhabited island nearby from Turkey for a year, for the purpose of a scientific experiment. Greece was consulted, but probably out of courtesy, since it wasn't any of their business. I don't think that's a problem! It would be like Hawaii taking over an empty island from the US for a while…

PROS
An excellent example of well-thought-out, well-written whatthefuckery!
I particularly like the 2312-Eris events listed in the chart… LOL
The fact that for a long time Eris events were thought to have nothing to do with each other. Ingenious!

CONS
I hesitated before reading your 'What's going on?' collapsible - was afraid it would ruin the thing for me. Well, a bit. I'd rather stick to not knowing who the benefactor is, and for someone (like me) not familiar with Dmatix works, there is no way to guess.. and no need to either.

Verdict: upvote, gladly!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Taken as a good, in-universe writeup of strange, hyperdimensional geopolitical fuckery, this gets the upvote. Even though I doubt very much that anyone would be able to get either Greece or Turkey at the moment to willingly give up sovereignty of anything for any period for any reason on Cyprus short of armed force, this is written with a pretty keen awareness of real-world circumstances. This is a good aid for reader immersion, and thus this demonstrated the feel that good, shorter articles tend to have. A solid exercise in the format.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fabricating a disease rather than making a cover story?

Technically, the cover story is to make up a fake disease. It does matter if it's true, so long as people buy it. 2 + 2 = 5, as they say.

Keeping a barely-recognized country sovereign to contain a skip?

And still barely-recognised as of 2015. While the appropriate action would suggest making TRNC be recognised throughout the international community, that does not happen that much. The Foundation don't really (IMO) have that kind of power to really move governments (non-UN countries would be easier for the Foundation to work in), which shows the limits of even the Foundation.

EDIT: Taking in your feedback, you feel as though the Foundation literally create cyclones and diseases to explain things (which is not my intent). Amended accordingly, while keeping word count the same.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, I dunno. Keeping a barely-recognized country sovereign to contain a skip? Kinda ehh, mnyeh, rlyeh? Fabricating a disease rather than making a cover story? That's just weird, not to mention overkill. The base idea as described in the spoilers is interesting, but doesn't really come through in the article as interesting.

item.title: Re: Word Count Note, Thanks, Attribution and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
The benefactor is actually Saturn Deer, a character crafted by Dmatix.

From the spoilers. Amazingly, they are two different things altogether (even I was initially confused by it).

On that note, Saturn Deer pre-dates THE DEER, right?

item.title: Re: Word Count Note, Thanks, Attribution and Other Matters
item.content:encoded: Djoric made SCP-2845, THE DEER from Saturn. Dmatix made Saturn Deer. Two completely different characters, despite the similar names.

item.title: Re: Word Count Note, Thanks, Attribution and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
spoilers
– hide block

Isn't THE DEER Djoric's? Or did DMatix create the character and Djoric turned it into a skip?

item.title: Re: Word Count Note, Thanks, Attribution and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
Yup… originally, I omitted what caused this whole thing… and then I really want to have an external source involved.

Pan is a royal twit, so him finding out how to cross dimensions and instil control over pieces of those dimensions will be too much for him.

P.S. Thanks for breaking my belief that I have crafted such a self-explanatory SCP that one can only "vote-and-go".1

Footnotes
1. It's rather flawed since I do FAQs for my articles all the time.

item.title: Re: Word Count Note, Thanks, Attribution and Other Matters
item.content:encoded: On part of the spoiler: I knew he had to be involved in this somehow.

item.title: Word Count Note, Thanks, Attribution and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
Word count is: 500

As proof:

+ show block
– hide block

Firstly, I would like to thank Ms. Light and other contest organisers for bringing this lovely contest to the site. This has been a fun and engaging experience for me. And I have used every single word I can get away with.

Secondly, I would like to thank the many people who had reviewed this draft such as azzleflux, Decibelles, OZ Ouroborus etc. (there were too many whom I showed to).

Special thanks to psul for his review, his faith in this article and bothering to check the word count on his initiative even when he was checking out another article.

Lastly, I would like to thank my patience for waiting for another day to post this since I made a last minute change that I feel is for the best and justifies this short article here.

The image is credited to Abser of Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:TRNC_location.png) via CC BY-SA 3.0.

Date format used here is dd/mm/yyyy.

Since I do this for all my articles, what's going on?

+ show block
– hide block

King Pan, in summary, is a multiversal feudal king that controls multiple dimensions' iterations of "Cyprus". Since he is based in the Kyrenias as his "capital", it is established as the link to all other dimensions' Cyprus. i.e. King Pan controls a state that currently has the Kyrenias as part of its territory.

The benefactor is actually Saturn Deer, a character crafted by Dmatix. Go search the site for his Saturn Deer works and support them too.

If you take note of the dates, the update was made after the Annam Plan. And no, the Foundation have no part in the outcome. I mean, the Annam Plan is manned by the UN (parent organisation of their no. 1 rival – GOC). Besides, the Foundation is an organisation of mostly R&D, intelligence and military elements. Diplomats would not be their strong point.

Anyway, the status of Cyprus may be purely of the people's will or something else?

As for the shortness, it is implied to be from lower clearance level (Level 2, in fact). The information presented here are solely on a need-to-know basis, enough for the in-universe reader to fulfil his job scope.
